## Summary

Both the read-time and write-time sanitizers were altering **manuscript-owned content** — a governance violation. If an author literally wrote "studies are mixed on the success of safe injection sites" in their manuscript, the sanitizer rewrote their quoted text to "scene-specific evidence is mixed", corrupting the evidence trail.

**Governance rule now enforced:**

```
Allowed to sanitize (RG editorial):     NOT allowed to sanitize (author-owned):
─────────────────────────────────────    ──────────────────────────────────────
overview.one_paragraph_summary           rec.anchor_snippet
overview.top_3_strengths[*]              evidence[*].snippet / evidence_snippets[*].snippet
overview.top_3_risks[*]                  any preserved manuscript excerpts
criteria[*].rationale
rec.action, rec.specific_fix
rec.expected_impact, rec.mechanism
rec.reader_effect, rec.symptom
rec.mistake_proofing
quick_wins[*], strategic_revisions[*]
evidence[*].note (editorial annotation)
```

**Changes:**

- `downloadReadTimeSanitizer.ts`: Remove `anchor_snippet` and `evidence_snippets[*].snippet` from sanitization. Keep `.note` (editorial). Add governance doc comment.
- `persistEvaluationResultV2.ts`: Same — remove `anchor_snippet` and `evidence[*].snippet` from write-time sanitization.
- Tests: 2 new governance tests proving manuscript content is preserved while editorial fields are still sanitized.
